---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# shapeindices

<!-- badges: start -->
<!-- badges: end -->

The goal of shapeindices is to create 3 convexity and compactness indices for sf polygons. It does so by triangulating the polygon as well as creating a convex hull. It accounts for holes and multi-polygons

## Installation

You can install the development version of shapeindices 

``` r
devtools::install_github(nkaza/shapeindices)
```

## Example

Here is a basic example of usage.

```{r example}
library(shapeindices)

square <- sf::st_polygon(list(rbind(c(0,0), c(10,0), c(10,10), c(0,10), c(0,0))))
moment_of_inertia_index(square)
convexity_index(square)
hull_ratio_index(square)
```
